Title: Correlated UAC bypass and callback chain
Description: Telemetry forms a high-confidence chain: auto-elevated system binary, elevated payload from a suspicious path, then outbound connectivity from that payload.
Recommendation: Escalate immediately, isolate the endpoint, and collect process tree plus memory telemetry for the elevated payload.

Title: In-memory payload execution pattern
Description: Source contains a classic shellcode runner sequence: executable allocation, memory copy, and thread creation.
Recommendation: Hunt for memory allocations with execute permissions combined with thread creation and immediate network activity.

Evidence.Snippet: static extern IntPtr VirtualAlloc(IntPtr lpAddress, uint dwSize, uint flAllocationType, uint flProtect);
static extern IntPtr CreateThread(IntPtr lpThreadAttributes, uint dwStackSize, IntPtr lpStartAddress,

Title: Suspicious outbound connection from staged payload
Description: A payload from a suspicious path initiated a network connection, consistent with reverse-shell or callback behavior.
Recommendation: Correlate with elevation events and block outbound traffic from unexpected administrative payloads.

Title: Elevated payload launched from non-standard path
Description: A high-integrity process was launched from a path that is unusual for trusted administrative software.
Recommendation: Alert on high-integrity child processes that execute from temp, user-writable, or hardcoded staging directories.

Title: Executable RWX memory allocation indicators
Description: Source references executable writable memory, which is a strong indicator for shellcode runners and packers.
Recommendation: Use EDR or ETW telemetry to flag RWX allocations made by unsigned or newly launched processes.

Evidence.Snippet: static extern IntPtr VirtualAlloc(IntPtr lpAddress, uint dwSize, uint flAllocationType, uint flProtect);

Recommendation: Monitor process creation chains that involve auto-elevated Windows binaries and validate parent-child ancestry for unexpected payload launches.
